Retrieved December 11, 2019.</ref> Even the U.S. Democratic Party worried about the Labour Party's anti-Semitism.<ref>Kelly, Laura (December 12, 2019). [https://thehill.com/homenews/news/474204-democrats-worried-by-jeremy-corbyns-uk-rise-amid-anti-semitism Democrats worried by Jeremy Corbyn's UK rise amid anti-Semitism]. ''The Hill''. Retrieved December 12, 2019.</ref>

===U.S. Democratic Party===
The [[Democratic Party]] is becoming increasingly anti-Semitic.<ref>Murphy, James (March 8, 2019). [https://www.thenewamerican.com/usnews/congress/item/31685-do-democrats-have-an-anti-semitism-problem Do Democrats Have an Anti-Semitism Problem?] ''The New American''. Retrieved March 8, 2019.</ref> ''The problem is, instead of the Left condemning and strongly opposing these attacks, antisemitism has found a home in the Democratic Party and has become a shameful problem that the Left must confront.''<ref>https://www.jpost.com/opinion/the-rise-of-antisemitism-in-the-left-and-in-america-637017 The rise of antisemitism on the Left and in America] Matthew Wearp, ''JPost'', July 31, 2020</ref> Far-left Democrat Arab congresswoman [[Rashida Tlaib]] hung a map in her congressional office with a sticky note calling [[Israel]] "Palestine."<ref>Multiple references:
*Kraychik, Robert (January 4, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/politics/2019/01/04/rashida-tlaib-new-office-map-israel-renamed-palestine/ Rashida Tlaib’s New Office Map: Israel Renamed ‘Palestine’]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved January 5, 2019.
*O'Neil, Tyler (January 4, 2019). [https://pjmedia.com/trending/israel-replaced-with-palestine-on-rashida-tlaibs-office-map/ Israel Replaced With Palestine on Office Map of Congresswoman Who Called Trump a 'Motherf**ker']. ''PJ Media''. Retrieved January 5, 2019.
*[https://www.jns.org/new-congresswoman-tlaib-displays-map-with-israel-replaced-by-palestine/ New Congresswoman Tlaib displays map with Israel replaced by ‘Palestine’]. ''Jewish News Syndicate''. January 3, 2019. Retrieved January 5, 2019.</ref> She earlier endorsed the anti-Israel [[Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions|BDS movement]].<ref>Chasmar, Jessica (December 4, 2018). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2018/dec/4/rashida-tlaib-newly-elected-democrat-endorses-anti/ Democratic Rep.-elect Rashida Tlaib endorses anti-Israel BDS movement]. ''The Washington Times''. Retrieved January 5, 2019.</ref> In addition to these anti-Semitic actions, Tlaib also made a bigoted tweet accusing Jewish-Americans of having divided loyalties, an attack frequently made by anti-Semites.<ref>Multiple references:
*Starr, Penny (January 7, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/politics/2019/01/07/democrat-rashida-tlaib-blasted-for-antisemitic-tweet-accusing-dual-loyalty-israel-bds/ Democrat Rashida Tlaib Blasted for Antisemitic Tweet: ‘They Forgot What Country They Represent’]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved January 7, 2019.
*Re, Gregg (January 7, 2019). [https://www.foxnews.com/politics/rashida-tlaib-accused-of-anti-semitic-slur-days-after-profane-anti-trump-tirade Rashida Tlaib accused of anti-Semitic slur, days after profane anti-Trump tirade]. ''Fox News''. Retrieved January 8, 2019.</ref> In January 2019, Tlaib willingly photographed herself with a [[Hezbollah]]-praising activist to also denied Israel's right to exist.<ref>Multiple references:
*Klein, Philip (January 14, 2019). [https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/democrat-rep-rashida-tlaib-photographed-with-pro-hezbollah-activist-who-says-israel-has-no-right-to-exist Democrat Rep. Rashida Tlaib photographed with pro-Hezbollah activist who says Israel has no right to exist]. ''Washington Examiner''. Retrieved January 14, 2019.
*Richardson, Valerie (January 14, 2018). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2019/jan/14/pro-hezbollah-activist-posts-photos-rashida-tlaib/ Pro-Hezbollah activist posts photos with Rashida Tlaib at swearing-in ceremony]. ''The Washington Times''. Retrieved January 14, 2018.
*Brest, Michael (January 14, 2019). [https://dailycaller.com/2019/01/14/israel-palestine-tlaib-picture-extremist-abbas-hamideh/ Anti-Israel Extremist Says He was At Tlaib's Private Dinner After Swearing In]. ''The Daily Caller''. Retrieved January 15, 2019.
*Danan, Deborah (January 15, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/middle-east/2019/01/15/democrat-rep-rashida-tlaib-poses-with-pro-hezbollah-rabid-anti-israel-activist/ Rashida Tlaib Poses with Pro-Hezbollah Activist]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved January 15, 2019.</ref>

In 2019, the Democratic Party appointed far-left Islamist Representative [[Ilhan Omar]] – who takes very anti-Israel positions – to the House Foreign Affairs Committee.<ref>Multiple references:
*Pollak, Joel B. (January 17, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/politics/2019/01/17/nancy-pelosi-appoints-anti-israel-radical-ilhan-omar-to-house-foreign-affairs-committee/ Nancy Pelosi Appoints Anti-Israel Radical Ilhan Omar to House Foreign Affairs Committee]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved January 17, 2019.
*Lowe, Tiana (January 17, 2019). [https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/house-democrats-reward-an-anti-semitic-conspiracy-theorist-with-a-place-on-the-foreign-affairs-committee House Democrats reward an anti-Semitic conspiracy theorist with a place on the Foreign Affairs Committee]. ''Washington Examiner''. Retrieved January 17, 2019.
*Kirkwood, R. Cort (January 18, 2019). [https://www.thenewamerican.com/usnews/politics/item/31221-muslim-rep-suggests-graham-is-compromised-pelosi-gives-her-seat-on-foreign-affairs-committee Muslim Rep. Suggests Graham Is “Compromised"; Pelosi Gives Her Seat on Foreign Affairs Committee]. ''The New American''. Retrieved January 18, 2019.
*Tapscott, Mark (January 21, 2019). [https://www.theepochtimes.com/gop-punishes-steve-king-anti-semitic-dem-gets-choice-committee_2774145.html GOP Punishes Steve King, Anti-Semitic Dem Gets Choice Committee]. ''The Epoch Times''. Retrieved January 21, 2019.</ref> Later, in August 2019, Omar attacked Israel because of the Palestinian government's decision to ban LGBT activities.<ref>Richardson, Valerie (August 20, 2019). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2019/aug/20/ilhan-omar-blasts-israel-after-palestinian-authori/ Ilhan Omar blasts Israel after Palestinian Authority cracks down on LGBT rights]. ''The Washington Times''. Retrieved August 20, 2019.</ref>

Leftist U.S. representative Betty McCollum, referred by some as "[[BDS]] Betty",<ref>[https://www.stopantisemitism.org/antisemite-of-the-week-2/antisemite-of-the-week-betty-mccollum-aka-bds-betty Antisemite Of The Week: Betty McCollum - aka BDS Betty]</ref> a [[DFL]] [[anti-Zionist]] similar to [[Ilhan Omar]], has called [[Israeli]] policies "[[apartheid]]".<ref>[https://www.questia.com/magazine/1P4-2137104783/rep-betty-mccollum-it-s-called-apartheid Rep. Betty McCollum: “It’s Called Apartheid”]</ref> McCollum in mid-February 2020 lashed out at the [[AIPAC]] for attacking her record, ironically accusing the pro-Israel organization of "anti-Semitism".<ref>Multiple references:
*[https://www.minnpost.com/national/2020/02/whats-behind-rep-betty-mccollums-most-recent-run-in-with-aipac/ What’s behind Rep. Betty McCollum’s most recent run-in with AIPAC]
*[https://bringmethenews.com/minnesota-news/rep-betty-mccollum-slams-aipac-after-ad-suggests-shes-worse-than-isis Rep. Betty McCollum slams AIPAC after ad suggests she poses 'more sinister threat' than ISIS]
*[https://freebeacon.com/politics/dem-rep-slams-aipac-as-hate-group/ Dem Rep Slams AIPAC as ‘Hate Group’]</ref> She had been one of seventeen members of Congress in July 2019 to vote against a bipartisan resolution condemning the [[BDS]] movement.<ref>Two references:
*[https://www.cnn.com/2019/07/23/politics/no-votes-house-resolution-israel-boycott-movement/index.html Who voted ‘no’ on the House resolution opposing Israel boycott movement]
*[https://forward.com/fast-forward/428179/congress-bds-aoc-tlaib-omar/ Here Are The 17 Members Of Congress Who Voted Against Condemning BDS]</ref> She had also voted in 2006 against an anti-[[Hamas]] bill;<ref>[https://forward.com/news/1009/lawmaker-aipac-feud-after-fight-over-hamas-bill/ Lawmaker, Aipac Feud After Fight Over Hamas Bill]</ref> the terrorist organization is known for their despicable use of children as human shields.<ref>[https://nypost.com/2014/08/05/hamas-manual-details-civilian-death-plan-israel/ Hamas’ disturbing ‘human shields’ manual]</ref><ref>[https://nypost.com/2015/05/02/un-report-outlines-how-hamas-used-kids-as-human-shields/ UN report outlines how Hamas used kids as human shields]</ref><ref>[https://www.channel4.com/news/factcheck/factcheck-hamas-civilians-human-shields Does Hamas use civilians as human shields?]</ref><ref>[https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2018/may/31/the-inhumanity-of-hamas-human-shields/ The inhumanity of Hamas' human shields]</ref> McCollum received recognition from a pro-[[Palestinian]] group in mid-September 2020,<ref>[https://www.middleeasteye.net/news/us-congresswoman-receives-champion-palestinian-rights-award US congresswoman Betty McCollum receives award for Palestinian children advocacy]</ref> and joined anti-Semitic House colleagues [[Ilhan Omar]] and [[Rashida Tlaib]] a month later at a conference with terrorist funders.<ref>Multiple references:
*[https://clarionproject.org/omar-tlaib-mccollum-participate-american-muslims-for-palestineamist-conference/ Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib & Betty McCollum Participate in Conference with Terror Funders, Antisemites]
*[https://drrichswier.com/2020/12/03/ilhan-omar-rashida-tlaib-betty-mccollum-participate-in-conference-with-terror-funders-antisemites/ Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib & Betty McCollum Participate in Conference with Terror Funders, Antisemites]
*[https://freebeacon.com/democrats/omar-and-tlaib-headline-anti-israel-hatefest/ Omar and Tlaib Headline Anti-Israel Hatefest]</ref> She has been dubbed a "useful idiot" for [[Hamas]].<ref>[https://www.frontpagemag.com/fpm/2020/09/us-rep-betty-mccollum-useful-idiot-hamas-joe-kaufman/ US Rep Betty McCollum – ‘Useful Idiot’ for Hamas]</ref>

Many left-wing anti-Semites claim to love Jews while condemning Zionism and Israel, though their comments in relation to Judaism shown otherwise.<ref>Brog, David (January 21, 2019). [https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/op-eds/beware-the-fork-tongued-anti-semites-who-claim-to-love-jews Beware the fork-tongued anti-Semites who claim to love Jews]. ''Washington Examiner''. Retrieved January 21, 2019.</ref>

In April 2019, ''[[The New York Times]]'' published an anti-Semitic cartoon depicting President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister [[Benjamin Netanyahu]]. The cartoonist had a history of drawing anti-Semitic cartoons.<ref>Morton, Victor (April 28, 2019). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2019/apr/28/antonio-moreira-antunes-new-york-times-cartoonist-/ Cartoonist whose work was pulled by New York Times had pattern of anti-Semitic tropes]. ''The Washington Times''. Retrieved April 29, 2019.</ref><ref>Spiro, Amy (April 29, 2019). [https://www.jpost.com/Israel-News/Cartoonist-behind-NYT-caricature-has-history-of-controversy-588074 Cartoonist Behind 'NYT' Caricature has History of Controversy]. ''The Jerusalem Post''. Retrieved April 29, 2019.</ref> The American Jewish Committee did not accept the NYT's non-apology.<ref>Danan, Deborah (April 28, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/middle-east/2019/04/28/american-jewish-committee-to-ny-times-apology-not-accepted-on-anti-semitic-cartoon/ American Jewish Committee to NY Times: ‘Apology Not Accepted’ on Anti-Semitic Cartoon]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved April 28, 2019.</ref> The newspaper only apologized several days later despite having the chance to do so earlier.<ref>Boyle, Matthew (April 28, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/the-media/2019/04/28/under-fire-new-york-times-finally-apologizes-antisemitic-cartoon-after-days-refusing-do-so/ Under Fire, New York Times Finally Apologizes for Antisemitic Cartoon After Days of Failing to Do So]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved April 29, 2019.</ref> If it could not get any worse, the NYT published another anti-Semitic cartoon a few days later.<ref>Boyle, Matthew (April 29, 2019). [https://www.breitbart.com/the-media/2019/04/29/new-york-times-prints-another-anti-israel-cartoon-amid-antisemitism-scandal-rocking-newspaper/ New York Times Prints Another Anti-Israel Cartoon Amid Antisemitism Scandal Rocking Newspaper]. ''Breitbart News''. Retrieved April 29, 2019.</ref>

After despicable NYT's Catie Edmondson's piece on Sept. 23, 2021 (following radicals' attempt to halt improvement for defense system Iron Dome for Israeli civilians from terror Arab-Palestinians), NYT was slammed:<ref>
Elder of Ziyon (@elderofziyon) Tweeted [https://twitter.com/elderofziyon/status/1441347307337764871?s=20 Sep 24, 2021]:
To the @NYTimes, Israel haters have "principles" while the Israel lobby has "power." https://t.co/o4h1mU0cyy</ref> ''To the @NYTimes, Israel haters have "principles" while the Israel lobby has "power."'' And major news Syndicate  asked ''Will the Nytimes editors admit to echoing  Antisemitic tropes about (supposed) Jewish "power" used against good, and apologize?''<ref>Jewish News Syndicate (@JNS_org) Tweeted ([https://twitter.com/JNS_org/status/1442206867753738247?s=20 Sep 26, 2021]):
Will the  @nytimes editors admit to echoing #antisemitic tropes about Jewish power used against good, and apologize? @deanbaquet @JosephKahn 
https://t.co/NmwmsLbr3b</ref>

Various anti-Semitic attacks in the U.S. have disproven the left-wing claim that anti-Semitism is only a "right-wing" or "white nationalist" trait.<ref>Bailey, Beth (December 30, 2019). [https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/latest-anti-semitic-attacks-make-it-clear-hatred-of-jews-doesnt-just-come-from-white-supremacists Latest anti-Semitic attacks make it clear: Hatred of Jews doesn't just come from white supremacists]. ''Washington Examiner''. Retrieved January 6, 2020.</ref>

In late March 2020 amidst the [[CCP virus in the United States]], [[NYC]] mayor [[Bill de Blasio]], sometimes referred to as [[Bill de Blowhard]], threatened a ''permanent closure'' of churches and synagogues that don't abide by his restrictive totalitarian orders.<ref>Two references:
*[https://pjmedia.com/news-and-politics/tyler-o-neil/2020/03/30/bill-de-blasio-threatens-to-permanently-close-churches-synagogues-if-they-meet-during-coronavirus-n383004 Bill de Blasio Threatens to 'Permanently' Close Churches, Synagogues if They Meet During Coronavirus]
*[https://townhall.com/tipsheet/leahbarkoukis/2020/03/30/de-blasio-threatens-to-close-churches-synagogues-permanently-for-holding-services-n2565954 De Blasio Threatens to Close Churches, Synagogues 'Permanently' For Holding Services]</ref> A month later, he tweeted a threat to arrest Jews who gather at synagogues<ref>Multiple references:
*[https://townhall.com/tipsheet/bethbaumann/2020/04/28/bill-de-blasio-is-bringing-the-hammer-down-on-nycs-jewish-community-n2567836 Both Sides Come Together to Condemn de Blasio's Anti-Semitic Orders]
*[https://thefederalist.com/2020/04/29/bill-de-blasio-should-resign-over-his-anti-semitic-tweet/ Bill De Blasio Should Resign Over His Anti-Semitic Tweet]</ref> following opposition to his anti-Semitic rhetoric in blaming the Jewish community for spreading the [[CCP virus]];<ref>[https://forward.com/fast-forward/445241/open-letter-from-jewish-organizations-politicians-castigates-de-blasio-for/ After Orthodox funeral, liberal Jews slam de Blasio for anti-Semitic tweet, threat of arrests]</ref> note the similarity between such and Hitler's blaming of Jews for systemic problems. He has even targeted Jewish schoolchildren under his mayorship.<ref>[https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/bill-de-blasio-targets-new-york-jewish-schoolchildren-with-new-anti-semitic-tweet Bill de Blasio targets New York Jewish schoolchildren with new anti-Semitic tweet]</ref> On September 9, 2020, [[left-wing]] Democrat [[Max Rose]] released an ad calling de Blasio "''the worst mayor in the history of New York City.''"<ref>Two references:
*[https://thehill.com/homenews/house/515738-democrat-releases-15-second-ad-calling-de-blasio-worst-mayor-in-history-of-new Democrat releases 15-second ad calling de Blasio 'worst mayor in the history of New York City']
*[https://nypost.com/2020/09/09/democratic-rep-max-rose-calls-de-blasio-worst-mayor-in-history-of-nyc/ Democratic Rep. Max Rose calls de Blasio worst mayor in history of NYC]</ref>

In late October 2020, a [[Jewish]] family in [[New York]] who participated at a pro-Trump rally was attacked, with far-left rioters splashing paint on their car and pepper-spraying the children inside, leaving them traumatized.<ref>[https://www.breitbart.com/2020-election/2020/10/26/jewish-family-traumatized-by-pepper-spray-attack-on-jews-for-trump-caravan/ Jewish Family Traumatized by Pepper-spray Attack on ‘Jews for Trump’ Caravan]</ref><ref>[https://www.foxnews.com/politics/anti-protesters-attack-jews-trump-vehicle-convoy-new-york Family describes horror as violent maskless rioters throw rocks, attack 'Jews for Trump' convoy]</ref><ref>[https://www.thegatewaypundit.com/2020/10/jewish-trump-supporters-attacked-new-york-city-video/ Jewish Trump Supporters Attacked in New York City (Video)]</ref> Some at the rally were punched and had rocks thrown at them.<ref>[https://www.breitbart.com/politics/2020/10/25/watch-jews-for-trump-caravan-rolls-thru-new-york-city-punches-rocks-thrown/ WATCH: ‘Jews for Trump’ Caravan Rolls Thru New York City; Punches, Rocks Thrown]</ref> [[BLM]] rioters in [[Philadelphia]] around this time also harassed Jews<ref>[https://www.thegatewaypundit.com/2020/10/get-fk-get-fk-black-lives-matter-screams-jews-joined-philly-protest-video/ “Get the F**k Out! Get the F**k Out!” — Black Lives Matter Screams at Jews Who Joined Their Philly Protest (VIDEO)] '''(Warning: Article contains foul language)'''</ref> and screamed that their [[synagogue]]s were "of Satan".<ref>[https://www.breitbart.com/law-and-order/2020/10/28/watch-blm-protesters-target-jews-in-philadelphia-while-yelling-synagogue-of-satan/ WATCH: BLM Protesters Target Jews in Philadelphia While Yelling ‘Synagogue of Satan’]</ref>

===Durban hate fests===
By 2021, some 31 States have shunned<ref>Yoseph Haddad (@YosephHaddad) Tweeted ([https://twitter.com/YosephHaddad/status/1440691381366788107?s=20 Sep 22, 2021]):<br><i>
This is what the Durban Conference looks like: empty thanks to dozens of countries that refused to participate and who was left participating in this hate conference?

Countries like Iran, Syria, Afghanistan, China and North Korea ... and that says it all!  Disgrace! </i>https://t.co/VMjDlFiFJG</ref> the racist "anti racism" conference. The Festival of Hate.<ref>Gerald M Steinberg, "[https://www.jns.org/opinion/durban-iv-the-uns-festival-of-hate/ Durban IV: The UN’s festival of hate]", JNS, Sep 22, 2021.<br><i>
Now, as in 2001, many of those who claim to speak in the name of morality and law continue to support the perpetrators of inhuman brutality, and erase the victims of terror and injustice.</i></ref> Writer elaborates on: 'Twenty years after Durban, what we still get wrong about left-wing anti-Semitism.'<ref>D. Bernstein, "Twenty years after Durban, what we still get wrong about left-wing anti-Semitism," JNS, Sep 26, 2021.<br><i>
Talking about the anti-Semitism at Durban without reference to postcolonialist ideology is like talking about the attacks of Sept. 11 without reference to extreme Islamist ideology.
The Durban conference was a watershed moment for Jews around the world, a stark reminder that the deliverance from the forces of history was not yet in the offing. The world’s oldest hatred was alive and well, not just in the remnants of an unreconstructed Eastern Europe or the aggrieved masses of the Third World, but among the cosmopolitan classes of the West. Yet few people addressed a basic question about this revival of anti-Semitism: What was the underlying ideology driving the Jew-hatred at Durban? And, 20 years later, with a resurgence of left-wing anti-Semitism in the United States and Europe, many still haven’t figured out how a variant of that same virus generates anti-Semitism today.
</i></ref>

==Palestinian themed anti-Semitism and violence==
:{{See also|Omar Shakir|Palestinian themed anti-Semitism under the Biden junta}}
[[File:Floyd_Palestine.jpeg|right|300px|thumb|[[George Floyd]] depicted as a Palestinian martyr.]]
[[Palestinian-themed violence]] refers to violence perpetrated by Palestinian-Arab and other jihadis as well as [[leftwing]] radicals (with their own agenda).  Most ethnic Palestinian-Arabs do not support [[Hamas]] per se or their [[Iran]]ian benefactors. Like the [[Black Lives Matter]] movement, where most BLM supporters are not Black, a segment of alleged "protesters" are non-Palestinian [[extremist]]s.

Under the incompetence of the [[Biden junta]] in 2021, escalations in conflicts between [[Israel]] and the Arab "Palestinians" increased, and many Jewish Americans have been the victims of violent attacks in the United States.<ref>Chavez, Nicole; Gingras, Brynn; Sgueglia, Kristina (May 21, 2021). [https://www.cnn.com/2021/05/21/us/anti-semitic-attacks-new-york-los-angeles/index.html Anti-Semitic attacks are being reported in US cities as tensions flare over the Israeli-Palestinian conflict]. ''CNN''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>Frantzman, Seth J. (May 22, 2021). [https://www.jpost.com/diaspora/antisemitism/militia-like-pro-palestinian-gangs-attack-jews-across-us-west-analysis-668824 Militia-like pro-Palestinian gangs attack Jews across US, West - analysis]. ''The Jerusalem Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> Acting [[junta leader]] [[Joe Biden|Joseph Robinette "Joe" Biden]], who courted Jewish voters in the [[2020 U.S. presidential election]] by vowing to fight [[anti-Semitism]],<ref>Biden, Joe (January 2, 2020). [https://www.jta.org/2020/01/02/opinion/joe-biden-my-plan-to-fight-anti-semitism Joe Biden: My plan to fight anti-Semitism]. ''Jewish Telegraphic Agency''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> has turned his back on them amidst rising [[anti-Semitic]] [[hate crime]]s by remaining silent.<ref>Talcott, Shelby (May 21, 2021). [https://dailycaller.com/2021/05/21/joe-biden-silent-anti-semitism-attacks-america-israel/ Joe Biden Remains Silent On Wave Of Anti-Semitic Crimes In US]. ''The Daily Caller''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref>

[[File:Israel5-1.png|left|thumb|[https://hannity.com/media-room/listen-palestinian-tells-israeli-soldier-if-children-need-to-die-then-theyll-die/ "Palestinian" Tells Israeli Soldier ‘If Children Need to Die, Then They’ll Die’] (during op. 'Guardian of Walls' May/21)]]

Biden has praised the virulent [[anti-Semite]] [[Rashida Tlaib]],<ref>Boyer, Dave (May 18, 2021). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2021/may/18/rashida-tlaib-praised-joe-biden-being-fighter/ Biden heaps praise on Tlaib: 'Thank you for being a fighter']. ''Washington Times''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> who previously used a bigoted dogwhistle to smear supporters of Israel.<ref>[https://nypost.com/2019/01/07/rashida-tlaib-casually-dives-into-anti-semitism/ Rashida Tlaib casually dives into anti-Semitism]. ''New York Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> Tlaib and the rest of [[the Squad]] have spouted talking points by the terrorist group [[Hamas]],<ref>Cooper, Abraham; Moore, Johnnie (May 18, 2021). [https://www.newsweek.com/will-dems-rebuke-squad-parroting-hamas-talking-points-opinion-1592176 Will Dems Rebuke the Squad for Parroting Hamas Talking Points? | Opinion]. ''Newsweek''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> which uses human shields.<ref>[https://www.jewishvirtuallibrary.org/hamas-rsquo-s-human-shields Operation Cast Lead: Hamas’s Human Shields]. ''Jewish Virtual Library''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>Katz, Yaakov (March 15, 2010). [https://www.jpost.com/Israel/Hamas-used-kids-as-human-shields 'Hamas used kids as human shields']. ''The Jerusalem Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>[https://www.channel4.com/news/factcheck/factcheck-hamas-civilians-human-shields Does Hamas use civilians as human shields?]. ''4 News''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>Fredericks, Bob (August 5, 2014). [https://nypost.com/2014/08/05/hamas-manual-details-civilian-death-plan-israel/ Hamas’ disturbing ‘human shields’ manual]. ''New York Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>[https://nypost.com/2015/05/02/un-report-outlines-how-hamas-used-kids-as-human-shields/ UN report outlines how Hamas used kids as human shields]. ''New York Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref><ref>Wilson, Joe (May 31, 2018). [https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2018/may/31/the-inhumanity-of-hamas-human-shields/ The inhumanity of Hamas' human shields]. ''Washington Times''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref>


One thing was clear, the attackers were not wearing MAGA hats...<ref>[https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/anti-semitic-attacks-spread-like-wildfire-in-the-us-during-gaza-conflict Antisemitic attacks spread like ‘wildfire’ in the U.S. during Gaza conflict], PBS Newshour, May 24, 2021.<blockquote>... The span of these attacks, they spread like wildfire across the country. You mentioned a few, California, Arizona, Wisconsin, Illinois, Michigan, New York, New Jersey, South Carolina, Florida, acts of harassment and vandalism and violence.

So, number one, the span is much greater than what we have seen, but secondly the tone, the brazenness, the audacity of these assaults in broad daylight. We have seen people basically say, if you are wearing a Star of David, you must be a Zionist and you should be killed.

We have seen people hurling bottles and objects at homes with mezuzot on the door that were identifiably Jewish. We have seen people driving cars or marauding through Jewish neighborhoods and yelling, "We're going to rape your women," right, or yelling things like "Allahu akbar," and literally then wreaking physical violence on people.

And one of the incidents that was captured was in broad daylight in Times Square, a group of people beating and bloodying a Jewish man whose only crime was he was wearing a kippah, to the point where he was left unconscious in the street while people kicked him, bloodied him with like crutches. 

... Well, let's be clear. None of the people committing these crimes wearing MAGA hats, right?

The reality is.. I do believe that political language can have real world consequences. But this is very different kind of political language... in this case, we have people waving Palestinian flags and then beating Jewish people. And, in this case, you know what I might really draw a parallel to is the hate crimes committed against Asian Americans, where unhinged, fictionalized conspiracies about China first spouted by political leaders led to real-world consequences, as Asian Americans were attacked on the streets.

Well, today, we have unhinged, fictionalized conspiracies about Israel, that somehow the Jewish state is systematically slaughtering children or committing genocide. And then that leads to real-world attacks on Jewish people in the streets of America, on our campuses, in our communities.

So, although, again, the political tenor may be different, the real outcome is the same. And that's why we think people, regardless of where you are on the spectrum, need to speak out clearly and firmly and forcefully and say, in an unambiguous way, that anti-Semitism is unacceptable, because, again, this isn't activism. It's hate, and it should be called out as such.</blockquote>
</ref> 

At times, its crude violent attacks targeting Jews under the Palestine flag, banner were accompanied by Islamic themes such as ''Allahu Akhbar''.<ref>LA Jewish man chased by 2 vehicles waving Palestinian flags: 'They were yelling Allahu Akbar'
Jewish Los Angeles resident said he 'thought they were going to kill me'
By Yael Halon. Fox News, May 20, 2021. https://www.foxnews.com/us/la-jewish-man-chased-palestinian-flags-yelling-allahu-akbar</ref>


On May 20, 2021, violent Arab-[[Palestinian]]s in [[New York City]] brutally assaulted pro-Israel demonstrators in [[Times Square]].<ref>Sales, Ben (May 22, 2021). [https://www.jta.org/2021/05/21/united-states/antisemitism-in-new-york-city-what-we-know-about-thursdays-attacks Antisemitism in New York City: What we know about Thursday’s attacks]. ''Jewish Telegraphic Agency''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref> Among those beaten was a seventeen year-old Jewish teenager, who was punched in the head and arm. The perpetrators shouted anti-Semitic slurs including "f***ing Zionist". Several days previously, a Palestinian mob in [[Los Angeles]] attacked Jews at a restaurant.<ref>Multiple references:
*JTA (May 19, 2021). [https://www.jta.org/quick-reads/pro-palestinian-demonstrators-attack-jews-at-la-restaurant Pro-Palestinian demonstrators attack Jews at LA restaurant]. ''Jewish Telegraphic Agency''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.
*CBSLA Staff (May 19, 2021). [https://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2021/05/19/police-investigate-possible-jewish-hate-crime-attack-at-beverly-grove-restaurant/ Police Investigate Possible Jewish Hate Crime Attack At Beverly Grove Restaurant]. ''CBS Los Angeles''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.
*Lyman, Brianna (May 20, 2021). [https://dailycaller.com/2021/05/20/los-angeles-jewish-anti-semitic-attack-pro-palestine-mob-twitter-sushi-fumi/ Pro-Palestinian Group Attacks Jewish People Outside Of LA Restaurant, Video Appears To Show. Police To Investigate]. ''The Daily Caller''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.
*Golding, Bruce (May 20, 2021). [https://nypost.com/2021/05/20/pro-palestinian-mob-reportedly-attacks-jewish-patrons-in-la/ Pro-Palestinian mob reportedly attacks Jewish patrons at LA restaurant]. ''New York Post''. Retrieved May 22, 2021.</ref>


On May 20, 2021, group of six to eight Arab men confronted Luca Lewis of New York Red Bulls, the Arab mob threatened him with knives who asked if he was Jewish. 
<ref>[https://www.newsweek.com/luca-lewis-new-york-red-bulls-says-he-was-threatened-men-knives-who-asked-if-he-was-jewish-1593882 "Luca Lewis of New York Red Bulls Says He Was Threatened by Men With Knives Who Asked if He Was Jewish"], Daniel Villarreal, Newsweek, 5/21/21.''...a group of six to eight Palestinian men confronted him and his friend during their walk around New York City on Thursday night.''</ref>


[[File:Waseem-awawdah-1.jpg|left|thumb|[https://nypost.com/2021/05/21/jewish-man-beaten-during-wild-midtown-protests-afraid-to-wear-yarmulke/amp/ Waseem Awawdeh has been hit with multiple charges, including a hate crime][https://trib.al/yGl7f6t After his arrest said: 'I would do it again':]]]

[[File:Al-Alaheri.PNG|left|thumb|[https://lawandcrime.com/crime/suburban-new-york-man-charged-with-torching-jewish-school-and-synagogue-trashing-catholic-church-display/ Ali Alaheri - Suburban NY Man Charged With Torching Jewish School and Synagogue, Trashing Catholic Church Display]]]

[[File:Danial-Shaukat.jpg|left|thumb|
[https://nypost.com/2021/05/25/man-accused-of-hurling-threats-outside-jewish-synagogue-apologizes/amp/ Daniel Shaukat with others, attacked Jews and yelled to kill all J...s"]]]

On May 22, 2021, two Jewish teens in Brooklyn, New York, were surrounded by a mob who assaulted them after they refused to yell hate-filled and antisemitic phrases including "kill all the Jews." <ref>[https://www.newsweek.com/jewish-teens-new-york-assaulted-group-demanding-they-yell-kill-all-jews-1594008 Jewish Teens in New York Assaulted by Group Demanding They Yell 'Kill All the Jews'] Benjamin Fearnow, Newsweek, May 23, 2021.
''Two Jewish teens in Brooklyn, New York, were surrounded Saturday night by a mob of people who assaulted them after they refused to yell hate-filled and antisemitic phrases including "kill all the Jews," police said... Police said the suspects demanded that the two Jewish teens chant out various antisemitic phrases or face physical violence. After refusing, the teens were allegedly punched in the head and face numerous times, and one of the suspects put the 17-year-old victim in a chokehold.''</ref>

On May 22, 2021, near Chicago, a so-called "pro-Palestinian" was arranged to attack participants of a demonstration, with chants of 'Kill the J**s' and the Islamic call of "Khyber."<ref>“‘Kill the Jews’: Arab mob attacks pro-Israel rally in Illinois,” Arutz Sheva, May 24, 2021. https://www.israelnationalnews.com/News/News.aspx/306739
<blockquote>
A pro-Israel rally held outside of Chicago over the weekend was crashed by a mob of Arab protesters who attacked the pro-Israel demonstration and called for the slaughter of Jews.

The incident occurred on Sunday in downtown Naperville, a city of roughly 150,000 less than 30 miles west of Chicago….

In the week leading up to the event, the Instagram account created for the march received over 1,300 comments containing vile anti-Semitism, hate speech, calls for violence at the event, and death threats toward the organizers said Daniel Raab, one of the people behind the rally... a counter-demonstration, dubbed the “Naperville Protest for Palestine”, was later planned, “with the explicit intention to intimidate the Jewish run rally and march onto city hall to confront the 50 Jews and allies gathered for Israel. Violent intentions were expressed as the goal of the confrontations.”... gathering was interrupted when multiple trucks filled with men waving Palestinian flags drove by, swearing at the attendees and yelling “Free Palestine”….

“We could hear the mob chanting while they marched toward us ‘From the river to the sea Palestine will be free’, ‘Intifada Intifada long live the Intifada’, and ‘Khyber Khyber ya Yahood, Jaish Muhammed sa Yahood,’” said Raab. “They were cursing at us and saying to ‘kill the Jews’.”

“When we evacuated a car with four teenage boys, one Jew and three non-Jews from the Walk for Israel got caught in the mob at a traffic light. They were surrounded by the mob with people jumping on and into their car, spitting and punching.”..</blockquote></ref>

Another notable arrest in New York occured when an Arab, Waseem Awawdeh, who with others carried out a horrific lynching on a Jew wearing a kippah in Manhattan.<ref>[https://nypost.com/2021/05/21/jewish-man-beaten-during-wild-midtown-protests-afraid-to-wear-yarmulke/amp/ Waseem Awawdeh has been hit with multiple charges, including a hate crime], NY Post, May 21, 2021</ref>
Another Arab, Daniel Shaukat was arrested for him with others attacking Jews in Brooklyn, New York, while yelling anti-Semitic statements including "Kill all J**s".
<ref>[https://abc7ny.com/20-year-old-arrested-in-back-to-back-attacks-against-jewish-victims/10693468/ 20-year-old Daniel Shaukat arrested in a series of attacks on Jewish victims in Brooklyn.] Eyewitness News, May 25, 2021. ''Police say Shaukat and two others parked outside the synagogue shouted an anti-Semitic threat. They allegedly shouted, “Free the Palestinians! Kill all the Jews!” with four male victims standing outside the place. Two of the suspects got out of the car and began hitting the front door of the synagogue. They couldn’t get inside. Police say they kicked and destroyed the side mirrors of a nearby car on their way back to the car. Shortly thereafter, another bias incident may have occurred at Kensington’s Ocean Parkway ... Shawkat and another man approached the victim and his cousin and demanded that they “release Palestine.” Shawkat is said to have hit the back of his head with a suction cup when the victim tried to stop Shawkat from hitting his cousin. Next, a second man with a bat chased them to Ocean Parkway...''</ref><ref>[https://nypost.com/2021/05/25/man-accused-of-hurling-threats-outside-jewish-synagogue-apologizes/amp/ Man accused of hurling threats outside Jewish synagogue apologizes]. By Paul Martinka, Tina Moore and Jesse O’Neill, NYPost, May 25, 2021.  
''The Bensonhurst resident was allegedly among the young men who yelled “f**k the Jews,” “kill the Jews” and “free Palestine” on Saturday as worshippers gathered to pray. Shaukat was also involved in two other bias crimes, officials said... He is facing hate crime charges of aggravated harassment, and is expected to be arraigned later Tuesday or Wednesday morning''.</ref>

Two were arrested in the attack on Jews in Los Angeles, who ''threw bottles and chanted "death to Jews" and "free Palestine," and men got out of the vehicles and began asking who was Jewish'': Xavier Pabon (of Banning)<ref>[https://nypost.com/2021/05/23/arrest-made-in-assault-on-jewish-diners-outside-la-restaurant/ Arrest made in assault on Jewish diners outside Los Angeles restaurant] By Samuel Chamberlain, May 23, 2021. ''Xavier Pabon was arrested in connection to the assault on a group of Jewish diners at Sushi Fumi in Los Angeles.''</ref>
and Samer Jayylusi (of Whittier).
<ref>[https://abc7.com/anti-semitism-fight-arrest-la-restaurant/10693316/ Beverly Grove restaurant attack: 2nd suspect arrested after attack on Jewish men seen on video, LAPD says], abc7.com.
...''people in the caravan threw bottles and chanted "death to Jews" and "free Palestine," and men got out of the vehicles and began asking who was Jewish. 35-year-old Samer Jayylusi of Whittier, was arrested overnight Monday in Anaheim and booked on suspicion of assault with a deadly weapon, "with hate crime enhancements being sought."... police arrested Xavier Pabon, 30, of Banning, for allegedly taking part in the attack, which was one of two assaults on Jewish victims last week that sparked community outrage and calls for an end to hate-motivated violence in the city.''</ref>

===Incitement===

====Islamic and radical-left politicians====

[[File:Beaches.jpg|center|thumb|[https://elderofziyon.blogspot.com/p/eoz-posters-for-apartheid-week.html 'Apartheid Week' Posters]]]

Waves of physical attacks came after the radical left's insults, demonization<ref>Michelle Boorstein: "Jewish American groups appeal to Biden White House after string of antisemitic attacks related to Israel." May 21, 2021. https://www.washingtonpost.com/religion/2021/05/21/jews-antisemitic-attack-los-angeles-times-square-biden-israel-palestine
<blockquote>
Near Miami, a family was assaulted as they walked in Bal Harbour on Tuesday. The Orgen family was visiting from New Jersey when a car of people yelling “Free Palestine” and “We’re going to rape your wife,”...since May 8 and that the ADL this week reported finding 17,000 tweets between May 7 and May 14 with variations of the words “Hitler was right.”... Rubin, who handled Jewish outreach for Bernie Sanders’s 2020 presidential campaign, said the current level of vitriol in the United States against Jews is higher than he’s ever seen it. He pointed to an '''increased use of the term “apartheid” by progressive politicians and the appearance of White nationalists in the Palestinian cause'''... “There is language being used to describe Israel that is heading us on a path to arguments about delegitimization, that Israel should not exist, that the issue is that it exists,” said Rubin, executive director of the American Jewish Congress. “Words are being used about Israel that are incredibly unsettling to the Jewish community that go further, and in a direction that makes the community feel very vulnerable. And that vulnerability is not a good place to be..</blockquote></ref> of Israel such as by bigoted<ref>[https://www.jpost.com/diaspora/wiesenthal-releases-ten-worst-outbreaks-of-antisemiticanti-israel-cases-611310 Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib, Jeremy Corbyn top list of worst antisemites] ''Corbyn, violent antisemitism in the US, Germany, Italy, France led the list, compiled by the Simon Wiesenthal Center''. By Benjamin Weinthal, JPost, December 20, 2019.</ref> Arab Muslims [[Ilhan Omar]], [[Rashida Tlaib]],<ref>[https://www.azcentral.com/story/opinion/op-ed/2021/05/19/tlaib-omar-should-punished-their-anti-israel-statements/5127721001/ Democrats must punish Tlaib and Omar if they're truly serious about antisemitism] ''Reps. Rashida Tlaib and Ilhan Omar support policies that are considered antisemitic - and Democrats refuse to call it out. They should.'' Farley Weiss, May 19, 2021</ref> followed by [[AOC]]<ref>Hillel Neuer (@HillelNeuer) tweeted:
A congresswoman, that U.N. report accusing Israel of 'apartheid' was authored by 9/11 conspiracy theorist Richard Falk, who was denounced by his own UN chief Ban Ki-moon and by the UK for antisemitism, and it was commissioned by Syria, Libya and 18 other Arab states. #factsmatter https://twitter.com/HillelNeuer/status/1394077789767323648?s=20</ref> <font size=1>(which she tried to justify by relying on anti-Israel hacks such Omar Shakir<ref>https://canarymission.org/individual/Omar_Shakir</ref><ref>https://www.thejc.com/news/uk/human-rights-watch-israel-apartheid-report-condemned-as-fiction-1.516093</ref> and Hagai Elad of B'tselem,<ref>https://honestreporting.com/tag/btselem/</ref><ref>https://www.commentarymagazine.com/articles/noah-pollak/the-btselem-witch-trials/</ref><ref>[https://translate.google.com/translate?hl=en&sl=auto&tl=en&u=https://www.israelhayom.co.il/opinion/840857 '"B'Tselem", do not advance your agenda on my back.'] Yosef Haddad, Israel Hayom, January 16, 2021.</ref> both abusing Human rights banners/ positions for their extreme record long agenda)</font> and others.
Biden's press Secretary, [[Jen Psaki]] refused to denounce inflammatory anti-Israel rhetoric from House ‘Squad’.<ref>Press Secretary Psaki Pointedly Refuses To Denounce Anti-Israel Rhetoric From House Democrats. GOP War Room, May 20, 2021. https://youtu.be/Oq9LCUc4V3A</ref>

A journalist noted on May 24, 2021 that "still not a single elected Democrat--not one--has criticized the squad directly over their recent rhetoric. Not a single one."<ref>[https://twitter.com/SethAMandel/status/1396987670589579264?s=20 Seth Mandel (@SethAMandel) Tweeted]: ''Still not a single elected Democrat--not one--has criticized the squad directly over their recent rhetoric. Not a single one. I wish I could say Dems were insufficiently policing their caucus, but that would require any policing at all whatsoever.'' [May 24, 2021]</ref>

When [[Bernie Sanders]] was confronted about some liberals' use of the term "apartheid" <ref>https://www.thejc.com/news/uk/human-rights-watch-israel-apartheid-report-condemned-as-fiction-1.516093</ref><ref>https://aijac.org.au/op-ed/gross-distortions-of-hrw-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://www.janglo.net/item/LoHOoAAcUsr</ref><ref>https://www.ngo-monitor.org/reports/hrws-apartheid-demonization-bds-lawfare/</ref><ref>https://besacenter.org/perspectives-papers/the-new-blood-libel-of-medical-apartheid/</ref><ref>https://zionist.org.uk/the-btselem-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://www.wbez.org/stories/israeli-response-accusations-of-apartheid-slur-israel/b8b72c44-ad7f-4c3d-80d9-cc5aedf62410</ref><ref>https://honestreporting.ca/neil-macdonalds-apartheid-slur-is-inflammatory-and-unfounded</ref><ref>https://unwatch.org/u-n-s-mideast-agency-obsessed-israeli-apartheid/</ref>
<ref>https://ajn.timesofisrael.com/bob-carr-out-of-control/</ref><ref>https://www.newstatesman.com/culture/2016/02/i-grew-south-africa-so-believe-me-when-i-say-israel-not-apartheid-state</ref><ref>https://www.cjnews.com/perspectives/opinions/apartheid-slur-driven-hatred-lies
</ref><ref>https://www.washingtonpost.com/blogs/right-turn/wp/2014/05/19/explaining-john-kerrys-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://www.commentarymagazine.com/articles/jonathan-tobin/kerrys-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://brandeiscenter.com/the-apartheid-slur-an-intellectual-affront-and-obstacle-to-peace/</ref><ref>https://daphneanson.blogspot.com/2011/04/black-students-blast-israeli-apartheid.html</ref>
<ref>https://www.wsj.com/news/author/james-taranto?page=62</ref><ref>https://www.wbez.org/stories/israeli-response-accusations-of-apartheid-slur-israel/b8b72c44-ad7f-4c3d-80d9-cc5aedf62410</ref><ref>https://blogs.timesofisrael.com/israel-and-the-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://jpost.com/jerusalem-report/israel/the-apartheid-slur/</ref><ref>https://www.iol.co.za/amp/the-star/apartheid-slur-is-a-ploy-mr-kasrils-1135399</ref><ref>https://www.jpost.com/jerusalem-report/israel/the-apartheid-slur</ref><ref>[http://web.archive.org/web/20090828042304/http://cgis.jpost.com/Blogs/troy/entry/treat_the_apartheid_slur_the http://cgis.jpost.com/Blogs/troy/entry/treat_the_apartheid_slur_the]</ref><ref>[https://www.conservativewoman.co.uk/calling-israel-an-apartheid-state-is-both-racist-and-a-lie/ Calling Israel an apartheid state is both racist and a lie] By Karen Harradine and Paul T Horgan, Conservative Wonan, July 16, 2019</ref><ref>[https://www.cfi.org.uk/news.php?article=488 Israel and the Human Rights Distortion] Christian Friends of Israel, Apr 28, 2021</ref>) prompting vitriol which has led to an increase in anti-Semitic attacks, he shifted to blame (exclusively) "right wing" extremists.<ref>
5/23: Sanders, Gates, Honoré. ''This week on "Face the Nation," will the fragile [[ceasefire]] in the Middle East continue to hold? Plus, more hopeful signs that we're reaching the end of the pandemic.''
https://www.cbsnews.com/video/523-sanders-gates-honore/. 
J. Dickerson:<blockquote>
Let me ask you how this is played out here at home, the [[ADL]] says, there were 193 reports of anti-Semitic incidents this week, up from 131 the previous week, so that's during the period while the crisis began...
</blockquote>
B. Sanders:
<blockquote>
Anti-Semitism is rising in America, it is rising all over the world. We have to combat anti-Semitism. We have to combat the increase in hate crimes in this country, against Asians, against African-Americans, and against Latinos, so we have a serious problem of a nation which is being increasingly divided, being led by right wing extremists..
</blockquote>
J. Dickerson:
<blockquote>
There are a number of liberals who use the the word "apartheid".. a number of liberals in the house used that language.
<br>
The executive director of the American Jewish Congress, who handled Jewish outreach for your campaign, Joel Rubin has said that the use of that language has increased the level of vitriol that has contributed to this anti-Semitism...</blockquote></ref> At the same interview, while using the term "racists" Sanders could still not bring himself to use it on (incited by preachers and political leaders) Arabs inside Israel attacking Jews (since before [[Guardian of Walls]] operation began) nor on [[Hamas]]. Nor on some Muslims attacking Jews in the US under "Palestine" flag.

[[NYT]]'s columnists protested on the limited stand in face of the attacks; singling out Israel; incorrectness in descriptions and confronting reality to 'progressives who claim to be horrified by every form of prejudice':<ref>[https://www.nytimes.com/2021/05/24/opinion/anti-zionism-anti-semitism.amp.html Anti-Zionism isn’t anti-Semitism? Someone didn’t get the memo] Bret Stephens, The New York Times, May 24, 2021</ref>
<blockquote>
 ...it is especially despicable when Israel is singled out in ways that apply to no other country. To take just one example, when was the last time you heard of a campus demonstration or a call for boycotts and divestment in response to Turkey’s 47-year occupation of northern Cyprus or its routine bombardment, using U.S.-made jets, of Kurdish militants in Iraq?

The accusations made against Israel — stealing Palestinian land (despite the fact that Israel vacated the territory from which it was subsequently attacked) and wanton violence against Palestinian civilians, particularly children (despite the fact that Israel regularly warned its targets to vacate buildings before targeting them) — can’t help but make me think of ancient libels about Jewish greed and bloodlust.

Also echoing ancient libels is the idea that 11 days of fighting between Israel and Hamas somehow constitute a unique global horror, even as the world barely takes notice of the Taliban’s murder this month of 85 people at a school in Kabul. The anti-Semitic worldview is always Judeocentric, in the sense that it is obsessed with Jewish behavior as the supreme factor in domestic and international political life. The left has lately been awfully Judeocentric.

This '''ought to be whistling loudly in the ears of progressives who claim to be horrified by every form of prejudice.''' Instead, they have indulged an anti-Israel movement that keeps descending into the crudest forms of anti-Semitism. They remind me of a certain kind of Trump voter who would occasionally voice disgust at his most outrageous behavior, only to come back into alignment with him a few days later. After a while, it becomes clear that the outrage is cheap, if it isn’t simply fake.

Progressives will have to come to their own reckoning about what to do about the burgeoning anti-Semitism in their midst. As for Jews, they should take the events of the last few days less as an outrage than as an omen.</blockquote>

Journalist decried<ref>[https://translate.google.com/translate?hl=en&sl=auto&tl=en&u=https://www.ynet.co.il/judaism/article/H15tJ00ttO For the information of the Hadid sisters and their liberal friends: You have a responsibility to the harming of Jews] Natasha Kirtziuk-Gutman, Ynet, May 25, 2021</ref> the (daughters of Arab billionaire Mohamned) Hadid sisters' and their liberal friends' contribution to the increase in anti-Semitic attacks. The sisters who "have three times more followers than the total Jewish population in the world as of 2021," and that "when they spread their Fake News and increase antisemitism in the world - for some reason, everyone is silent. And if you were wondering what about the Palestinians - you just proved that this is not the conflict that interests you, because what is the connection between it - and beatings that a Jew gets in New York." She said. 
<blockquote>...these two supermodels blatantly circulated antisemitic posts, falsely portraying the Jews of Israel as white supremacists and colonialists, through fake news, blatant lies, infographics based on unreliable sources and a variety of techniques. With the goal to express support for the Palestinians, according to them.

They removed the posts later, either because of outside criticism or out of an understanding that what underpinned the posts they shared with many millions around the world, is of course pure anti-Semitism. But in simple terms: both have three times more followers than the total world Jewish population, after Adolf Hitler - that one, of those posts "Hit... was right" - succeeded in exterminating more than a third of the Jewish population in the Holocaust. This is after my family members have been attacked only because of their identity.</blockquote>


Journalist in [[Germany]] decried on Islamic hate crimes being hidden. Crime statistics on anti-Semitic attacks are manipulated to hide Muslim perpetrators, blatantly misleading for years and which automatically classify alleged anti-Semitic crimes as ‘right-wing’ motivated if they cannot be attribute to anything else.<ref>[https://medforth.biz/german-newspaper-complains-that-crime-statistics-on-anti-semitic-attacks-are-manipulated-to-hide-muslim-perpetrators/ German newspaper complains that crime statistics on anti-Semitic attacks are manipulated to hide Muslim perpetrators], Medforth, September 25, 2021.
<blockquote>
German politics misses the point when it comes to anti-Semitism. Journalist Lennart Pfahler is convinced of this. As he writes in the early edition of the newspaper “Welt am Sonntag” (September 26), Muslim anti-Semitism is still taboo in Germany.

Whoever names it must fear being called a “right-winger” or a “fig leaf” if they are Muslim themselves. This is a “criminal failure of discourse”: “Apart from the police crime statistics, which have been blatantly misleading for years and which automatically classify alleged anti-Semitic crimes as ‘right-wing’ motivated if they cannot be attributed to anything else, there is little to support the convenient thesis of the marginal phenomenon.

In a survey by the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights, 41 per cent of Jews in Germany said they felt most threatened by Islamic anti-Semitism.

Conservative politicians and Jewish associations regularly pointed out the problem. Too often, the response from “left-liberal columnists and Muslim activists” was appeasing. The debate about the broadcaster Nemi El-Hassan shows how much the fight against anti-Semitism has degenerated into a political question of opinion.

Background: German public broadcaster WDR wanted to hire the journalist as a presenter. After protests – among other things because of El-Hassan’s participation in an anti-Israel Al-Quds demonstration – WDR suspended its decision. Prominent voices now wanted to stall the reappraisal of the case, Pfahler writes. Especially for those Muslims who tried to address the issue in their own community, this defensive attitude was a slap in the face...</blockquote></ref>

====Islamic incitement - US====
The South [[Florida]] Muslim Federation disseminated a malicious campaign of vilification, distortion.<ref>[https://www.algemeiner.com/2021/05/20/dangerous-incitement-from-the-south-florida-muslim-federation/ Dangerous Incitement From the South Florida Muslim Federation] by Dexter Van Zile, The Algemeiner, May 20, 2021.
<blockquote>
 ...the video, protesters can be heard chanting, “Netanyahu, You Can’t Hide, We Charge You With Genocide,” “From the River to the Sea, Palestine Will Be Free!” and “Israel, What Do You Say? How Many Kids Have You Killed Today.”

At about eight and a half minutes into the video, which can be seen on Facebook here, a keffiyeh-wearing protester is shown displaying a sign that reads “IS...= NAZI/They Are Committing Genocide.”.... the letter, Kakli complains about the deaths of Palestinian children in the Gaza Strip, failing to mention that many of these deaths are caused by Hamas rockets that have fallen short and landed on the homes of Palestinians — or because Hamas uses civilians (and children) as human shields.

In his plea for peace in Jerusalem, Kakli makes no reference to the “TikTok Attacks” that took place in April, during which young Arab men recorded themselves attacking Orthodox Jews walking the streets of the Holy City and posted these videos on the Internet.

Kakli makes no reference to the ongoing incitement from the Al-Aqsa Mosque, where imams call for acts of violence against Jews, nor does he acknowledge the call from a leader of Hamas for his followers to buy a five-shekel knife and decapitate Jews with said knife.

In the story told by Kakli and promoted by the organization he leads, everything is Israel’s fault...
question for Deputy Hamze: Does the incitement his organization promotes against Israel make Jews in Florida more or less safe?
</blockquote></ref>
 

===Rallies===

In response, some 14 rallies were held around the States. Including under banner "United Against Anti-Semitism. United Against Terror. United for Us".<ref>Thousands rally for Israel at Ground Zero. Rally is one of 14 pro-Israel gatherings coast-to-coast. Arutz Sheva Staff, May 23, 2021.
https://www.israelnationalnews.com/News/News.aspx/306707</ref>

==Anti-capitalism, Anti-Americanism and anti-Semitism==
"[I]n North America," wrote Marx, the "practical domination of Judaism over the Christian world" had achieved what he called "its unambiguous and normal expression."<ref>Karl Marx, "On The Jewish Question," ''Deutsch-Französische Jahrbücher'', February 1844, reprinted in Karl Marx, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=TKdLO2jvbqsC Early writings]'' (Penguin, 1992) ISBN 0140445749, p. 232</ref> According to Sombart, “what we call Americanism is nothing else, if we may say so, than the Jewish spirit distilled.”<ref>Werner Sombart, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=P33VHF1aBAcC The Jews and Modern Capitalism]'' (E. P. Dutton, 1913), p. 44</ref>

“The anti-American left... is at least as anti-Jewish as the clerical conservative right, especially among those who have embraced a pronounced 'antifascist' and 'antiracist' credo.”<ref>Robert S. Wistrich, ''[http://www.ajc.org/atf/cf/%7B42D75369-D582-4380-8395-D25925B85EAF%7D/wistrich.pdf European Anti-Semitism Reinvents Itself],'' [The American Jewish Committee, 2002], p. 11</ref> "Anti-Americanism in the Islamic world and Anti-Americanism in Europe are in fact linked, and both bear an uncanny resemblance to anti-Semitism."<ref>Natan Sharansky, "On Hating Jews: The inextricable link between anti-Semitism and anti-Americanism," ''The Wall Street Journal'', November 17, 2003</ref>

"America and Jews are seen by many Europeans as paragons of a modernity they dislike and distrust: money-driven, profit-hungry, urban, universalistic, individualistic, mobile, rootless, inauthentic, and thus hostile to established traditions and values."<ref>"[http://www.jcpa.org/JCPA/Templates/ShowPage.asp?DRIT=3&DBID=1&LNGID=1&TMID=111&FID=624&PID=0&IID=658&TTL=European_Anti-Americanism_and_Anti-Semitism:_Similarities_and_Differences European Anti-Americanism and Anti-Semitism: Similarities and Differences]," An Interview with Andrei S. Markovits, ''Post-Holocaust and Anti-Semitism'' [Institute for Global Jewish Affairs], No. 16 [January 1, 2004]</ref> "Jews were blamed for the evils of capitalism in earlier centuries. Americans are blamed for globalization, that is, technological and economic change to the max, in our days."<ref>Josef Joffe, quoted in Mark O'Keefe, "[http://pewforum.org/Politics-and-Elections/Anti-Semitism-and-Anti-Americanism.aspx Anti-Semitism and Anti-Americanism]," Pew Forum on Religion & Public Life, May 5, 2006</ref> "America today has become the world's market-dominant minority," while "Jews are a market-dominant mionority in the Middle East."<ref>Amy Chua, ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=KSp1okOggpkC World on fire: how exporting free market democracy breeds ethnic hatred and global instability]'' [Random House, Inc., 2004] ISBN 0385721862, pp. 218, 229</ref>

Thus post-unification Germany, observed a journalist in 2005, is marked by "a poisonous cocktail of the three big As: anti-Americanism, anti-Semitism and anti-capitalism."<ref>Wolfgang Munchau, "[http://www.spectator.co.uk/essays/13684/part_2/antiamericanism-antisemitism-anticapitalism.thtml Anti-Americanism, anti-Semitism, anti-capitalism]," ''The Spectator'', May 21, 2005</ref> This association of Jews with America was illustrated in 2006, when German Neo-Nazis burned a copy of ''[https://books.google.com/books?id=y50yo2rnJHcC Anne Frank: Diary of a Young Girl]'' together [http://www.ejpress.org/article/news/germany/9582 with an American flag].

Despite the growth of liberal anti-Semitism, left-wingers who happen to be Jewish have largely ignored anti-Semitism on the Left.<ref>Markowicz, Karol (November 25, 2018). [https://nypost.com/2018/11/25/liberal-jews-are-still-turning-a-blind-eye-to-anti-semitism-on-the-left/ Liberal Jews are still turning a blind eye to anti-Semitism on the left]. ''New York Post''. Retrieved November 29, 2018.</ref>
